title: Tandoori Chicken

Chicken: 1 whole chicken (about 1.2–1.5 kg), cut into 8–10 pieces
First Marinade (for tenderization): 1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder (for color, mild heat)
Second Marinade (for depth & flavor): 250 g thick hung curd (strained yogurt, creamy texture)
2 tbsp mustard oil (or vegetable oil, but mustard oil gives authenticity)
1 tbsp Kashmiri red chili powder (adjust for heat)
½ tsp black salt (optional, gives restaurant taste)
1 tbsp kasuri methi (dry fenugreek leaves, roasted & crushed)
1–2 green chilies, finely chopped (optional, for spice kick)
Finishing: 2 tbsp melted butter or ghee (for basting)

Method.title: Prepare Chicken
steps: Make 2–3 deep slits in each chicken piece (to absorb marinade).
Rub with salt and lemon juice, rest for 15 minutes.

Method.title: First Marinade (Base flavor)
steps: Coat chicken with ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, and salt.

Method.title: Second Marinade (Creamy coating)
steps: Whisk yogurt until smooth. Add all second marinade spices and mustard oil.
Coat chicken evenly, massaging marinade into the slits.

Method.title: Cooking (Authentic Tandoor or Alternatives)
steps: Tandoor: Preheat until smoking hot. Skewer chicken and cook 12–15 minutes, basting with butter. Finish with a quick char.
Oven: Preheat to 250°C (480°F). Place chicken on a rack over a tray. Roast for 18–20 minutes. Then switch to broil/grill mode for 5 minutes to char.
Grill/BBQ: Medium-high heat, cover grill, cook 15–18 minutes, turning and basting.

Method.title: Finishing Touch
steps: Brush with melted butter/ghee just before serving.
Sprinkle with chaat masala, serve with onion rings, lemon wedges, and mint chutney.

Chef Tips: Mustard oil: Heat it lightly until smoking, then cool before using in marinade — removes raw smell and gives smoky flavor.
Hung curd: Thick curd ensures marinade clings and doesn’t drip off.
Kashmiri chili powder: Adds vibrant color without overpowering heat.
Smoke infusion (Dhungar method): If no tandoor, place a small piece of hot charcoal in a bowl inside the marinated chicken, pour a drop of ghee, cover for 5 minutes — adds smoky restaurant aroma.
